questions.question: Which country played host to the virtual Global Vaccine Summit?
questions.explanation: United Kingdom recently hosted the Global Vaccine Summit, which was held virtually due to the restrictions involved amidst the Covid-19 pandemic. Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi attended the conference and pledged $15-million as India’s contribution to the vaccines alliance GAVI. The British Prime Minister earlier urged all nations to pledge funds for vaccinations to save the lives of people across the world from outbreaks of infectious diseases in future.

questions.question: Which country passed a bill which criminalises the insulting of Chinese national anthem?
questions.explanation: The legislature of Hong Kong has recently approved a bill that makes it illegal to insult the Chinese national anthem. In Hong Kong, the pro-democracy camp sees the anthem bill as an infringement of freedom of expression whereas the pro-Beijing majority said the law was necessary to show appropriate respect for the anthem. If one intentionally abuses the “March of the Volunteers” anthem, he might face up to three years in prison and a fine of up to 50,000 Hong Kong dollars.

questions.question: What is the name of the initiative launched by the Environment Ministry to increase urban forest cover?
questions.explanation: The Union Environment Minister Prakash Javadekar launched the ‘Nagar Van’ or ‘Urban Forests’ programme to increase forest cover in urban areas. During the virtual celebration of World Environment Day 2020, themed as ‘Nature and Biodiversity’, the programme was launched with 200 corporations and cities across India. The Minister urged the people to actively participate in the programme to increase the tree cover in their areas. This year, the government’s target for tree plantation is 145 crores.

questions.question: Which city has been declared as the summer capital of Uttarakhand?
questions.explanation: As per the recent announcement from the Chief Secretary of Uttarakhand, the city of Gairsain in Chamoli district has been declared as the summer capital of Uttarakhand. The Governor of Uttarakhand Baby Rani Maurya also gave her assent to the decision. Gairsain is located about 270-km from the existing capital of Dehradun. It is expected that the declaration of summer capital in the region will accelerate development of the hilly areas.

questions.question: Which global organisation is headed by Tijjani Muhammad-Bande?
questions.explanation: The president of the UN General Assembly is Tijjani Muhammad-Bande, who is a Nigerian diplomat. He had also served as the Vice President of UNGA. Tijjani Muhammad-Bande has recently announced that world leaders will not be arriving in New York for their annual gathering in late September. This happens for the first time in the 75-year history of the UN due to the restrictions of the Covid-19 pandemic. The 193 heads of state and government will give speeches during the General Debate, the process of which will be announced.

questions.question: Which Indian state/ UT has launched a scheme for street vendors named “Mukhyamantri Shahri Path Vyavsayi Utthan Yojana”?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister of Madhya Pradesh Shivraj Singh Chouhan has launched a scheme called “Mukhyamantri Shahri Path Vyavsayi Utthan Yojana” The ‘Street Vendor Registration Portal’ was also launched by the Chief minister. Rs 300 crore was transferred to urban local bodies for implementing the scheme. Earlier, the state provided Rs 1,555 crore to 22,800 Gram Panchayats for the purpose of revamping the development in the state.

questions.question: Which country has launched plasma exchange network for COVID 19 treatment?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh has recently launched an online network to facilitate plasma exchange named ‘Shohojodha’. This enables an exchange between the recovered patients of COVID 19 and those undergoing treatment for Covid-19. However, the plasma therapy for treating Coronavirus patients in Bangladesh is under trial process and is awaiting final approval from the government.

questions.question: Who heads the working group constituted by the Reserve Bank of India, to review private bank shareholding norms?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has constituted an internal working group to review the guidelines on ownership and corporate structure of private sector banks. The working group will be headed by PK Mohanty, an Executive Director at RBI. The group will examine the licensing guidelines of banking and regulations on ownership of private sector banks. It will also review the regulations on holding of financial subsidiaries through a non-operative financial holding company.

questions.question: Which organisation has developed a sanitising chamber named ‘GermiKlean’ to disinfect uniforms of security forces?

options.label: C
options.text: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research

questions.explanation: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has developed a sanitising chamber named ‘GermiKlean’ to disinfect uniforms of security forces. GermiKlean is a dry heat treatment chamber which can sanitise 25 pairs of uniforms within 15 minutes. DRDO has also designed a sanitisation tunnel and a sanitisation mat for foot sanitisation of security forces. Delhi Police had earlier submitted their requirement for sanitising their uniforms, canes and helmets.

questions.question: Kalapani, Limpiyadhura and Lipulekh, that are seen in news recently, are located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The lower house of Nepal parliament, Pratinidhi Sabha passed bill to include India’s territories of Kalapani, Limpiyadhura and Lipulekh in Pithoragarh district of Uttarakhand in its new map. Nepal’s upper house of parliament has also endorsed proposal to update its political map that includes the Indian territories. However, India has claimed that the updated map is not based on historical facts and evidence and that the move is violative of the current understanding to hold talks on outstanding boundary issues.
